channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Iran's president insists Tehran does not seek a nuclear bomb and dangles US business opportunities
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Iran's president again pledged Wednesday that his nation is “not after a nuclear bomb” ahead of talks between Tehran and the United States, going as far as dangling the prospect of direct American investment in the Islamic Republic if the countries can reach a deal.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bryan Kohberger returns to court for hearing on pile of evidence he wants thrown out before trial
item.description: Idaho student murders suspect Bryan Kohberger will argue to have evidence withheld from trial and to have the death penalty taken off the table.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Mexico governor mobilizes National Guard to tackle crime emergency in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham on Tuesday declared a state of emergency in New Mexico’s largest city, saying that a significant increase in crime in Albuquerque warrants the help of the New Mexico National Guard.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia parents arrested after gun went off inside 3rd grader's backpack at school
item.description: A pair of Virginia parents were arrested after a gun in their third-grade son's backpack inadvertently went off during class, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stowaways found dead in JetBlue landing gear identified as teens
item.description: Two stowaways found dead in the landing gear compartment of a JetBlue plane earlier this year have been identified as teenagers, according to media reports. The teens were discovered Jan.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Indiana man caught raping teen near White River
item.description: According to court documents filed on Sunday in Delaware County, 30-year-old Arijan Urguni has been preliminarily charged with:
item.dc:creator: David Gay, NewsNation

item.title: Hearing could set rules for evidence and other details in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: Prosecutors and attorneys for a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students in 2022 are set to argue some of the final ground rules they want for Bryan Kohberger’s trial.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boone, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump botching tariff messaging because he lacks empathy: Cenk Uygur
item.description: "Why is [Trump] blowing it? Partly because he lacks empathy," Uygur told NewsNation's Leland Vittert on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs executive orders to boost coal, a reliable but polluting energy source
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed a series of executive orders aimed at boosting the struggling coal industry, a reliable but polluting energy source that’s long been in decline.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Outrage builds as video shows Idaho police shooting a knife-wielding autistic teenager
item.description: Idaho police officers opened fire from behind a chain-link fence just seconds after exiting their patrol cars and critically wounded a teenage boy — described by his family as nonverbal, autistic and intellectually disabled — as he stepped toward them with a knife, video from a witness shows.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, CLAUDIA LAUER and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Kentucky watches for surging rivers to recede so widespread cleanup can begin
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — After days of deluges overfilled rivers to near-record levels across Kentucky, residents were anxious Tuesday to return to their flooded homes and assess what's salvageable, even as stubbornly high waters kept some of them waiting even longer.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Microsoft fires employees who protested its support for Israel at 50th anniversary event
item.description: Two Microsoft employees were fired for protests condemning the company’s support for Israel at a 50th anniversary event last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Youth soccer coach charged in the death of 13-year-old found on Southern California road
item.description: A youth soccer coach has been charged in the death of a 13-year-old whose body was found on the side of a Southern California road.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A Florida man is executed for killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break was executed Tuesday evening. Michael Tanzi was pronounced dead at 6:12 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Keystone oil pipeline shutdown could quickly lead to higher gasoline prices
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — The nearly 2,700-mile Keystone oil pipeline was shut down Tuesday morning after it ruptured in North Dakota, halting the flow of millions of gallons of crude oil from Canada to refineries in the U.S. and potentially leading to higher gasoline prices.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and SARAH RAZA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Arroyo Colorado to be dredged after historic Rio Grande Valley floods
item.description: A Colorado company will design the dredging of a section of a South Texas waterway that flooded during historic recent rains, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commission says.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Human remains found in 1973 identified as missing Kansas teen
item.description: Human remains found near Garnett, Kansas, in 1973 have been positively identified more than 50 years later.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Agreement in works to settle Northwestern hazing lawsuits that led to football coach's firing
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former Northwestern University football players are finalizing an agreement with the school to settle lawsuits alleging hazing and abuse that led to longtime coach Pat Fitzgerald’s firing, a school spokesman and attorneys said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: GOP mood grows darker on Trump tariffs as markets give up their gains
item.description: Glimmers of optimism surrounding President Trump’s tariffs quickly faded on Tuesday as the White House dug in on the threat of new levies, the markets erased their small morning gains and Republican lawmakers grew increasingly frustrated with what they see as mixed messages coming from administratio
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration says New York must end Manhattan driving tolls this month
item.description: The Trump administration said Tuesday that it's not backing off its latest deadline for New York to end its $9 congestion toll on drivers entering the most traffic-snarled parts of Manhattan, despite a recent court filing that indicated the charge would remain at least through the summer. U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Huntsman Cancer Institute breaks ground on new $400M Utah County cancer center
item.description: The Huntsman Cancer Institute broke ground on its new location in Utah County. Here's the project details and timeline.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: Keystone pipeline shut down after North Dakota rupture
item.description: The affected segment has been isolated, and operations and containment resources have been mobilized to site,” the company said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-member of former Gambia dictator's military unit is on trial in the US for torture
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A man accused of torturing people suspected in a planned coup against Gambia’s longtime leader was a low-ranking private in the West African country’s military who risked torture and death himself if he disobeyed superiors, a lawyer for the defendant told jurors Tuesday in opening stat
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rapper 'Fat Flow' gets 33 years for kidnapping migrants
item.description: A judge has sentenced a rapper calling himself Fat Flow to 33 years and four months in prison for the 2023 kidnapping of seven migrants in Juarez, Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KTLA 5

item.title: Watch the world’s largest iceberg run aground near remote island in South Atlantic Ocean
item.description: Newly released satellite footage shows the world’s largest iceberg running aground near a remote island in the South Atlantic Ocean last month.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge tells government to provide evidence, or case against Columbia student Mahmoud Khalil is over
item.description: An immigration judge in Louisiana says she will decide later this week whether the government can continue to detain Mahmoud Khalil, the Columbia University activist facing deportation for his role in pro-Palestinian campus protests.
item.dc:creator: Sara Cline And Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Outrage builds as video shows Idaho police shooting a knife-wielding autistic teenager
item.description: A teenage boy described by his family as autistic, nonverbal and intellectually disabled remains in critical condition after being shot in his backyard by Idaho police last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Gene Johnson, Claudia Lauer And Mark Thiessen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Veterinarian goes missing after video showed him kicking horse
item.description: A Las Vegas veterinarian is missing following a viral video that shows him kicking a horse in the head. On Thursday, Dr. Shawn Frehner was paid to administer anesthetic shots for some horses at a Nevada property.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KTLA 5

item.title: Salt Lake Bees celebrate first home game at new Daybreak ballpark
item.description: The Bees will welcome the Reno Aces for a six-game homestand starting April 8, which will include giveaways, music, and activities for the whole family.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: More Americans call taxes unfair: Poll
item.description: More Americans say that the amount they will pay in federal income taxes this year is "unfair" than those who say their amount is reasonable, according to a new poll, and most say that corporations and wealthy people are not paying enough.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Photos: 'Jackson Magnolia' removed at White House, replaced with descendant sapling
item.description: The newest greenery added to the White House grounds came the day after one of the southern magnolia trees, which was said to have been planted by Jackson in the 19th century, was removed due to "serious safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US expected a big travel year, but overseas visitors
item.description: Olja Ivanic looked forward to welcoming some cousins from Sweden to her Denver home in June. Ivanic and the four travelers were planning to go hiking in Colorado and then visit Los Angeles and San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump assassination attempt suspect Ryan Routh sought rocket launcher from Ukraine: docs
item.description: Prosecutors revealed that Ryan Routh, the alleged would-be assassin of President Trump, tried to obtain military-grade arms from a contact in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Small plane skids off runway at Oregon airport, ends up in bay
item.description: A small private plane attempting to land at an Oregon airport skidded off the runway and into a body of water, leaving 5 people injured.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Foran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stephen A. Smith 'leaving all doors open' for presidential bid
item.description: Smith has a longstanding history of weighing in on politics, but has been particularly critical of the Democratic Party in the wake of President Trump’s success in the 2024 election, pushing for it to refocus on top issues for voters in last year’s election cycle including immigration and inflation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: House Democrats targeting 3 Florida Republicans in 2026 midterms
item.description: Last month, the National Republican Congressional Campaign Committee (NRCC) released its target list, which included 26 House Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas Sphere launching 'experience' around classic film
item.description: Sphere, the famed immersive Las Vegas entertainment arena, is set to debut two new "Sphere Experiences" later this year — and one is based on a beloved classic motion picture.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, ABC 4
